task_completion.description: Agent authenticates with the Nx Witness REST API to obtain a session token.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a curl command to 'https://localhost:7001/rest/v1/login/sessions' with credentials admin/Admin1234!, or a visible script containing this authentication logic.

task_completion.description: Agent retrieves the device IDs for the cameras from the API.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing a JSON response with camera names and IDs from '/rest/v1/devices', or a script containing logic to fetch and parse this endpoint.

task_completion.description: Agent updates the credentials for the 'Parking Lot Camera'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A PATCH request (via curl or script) to '/rest/v1/devices/{id}' setting the username to 'cam_admin' and password to 'P@rkL0t2024!Secure'.

task_completion.description: Agent updates the credentials for the 'Entrance Camera'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A PATCH request (via curl or script) to '/rest/v1/devices/{id}' setting the username to 'cam_operator' and password to 'Entr@nce2024!Secure'.

task_completion.description: Agent updates the credentials for the 'Server Room Camera'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A PATCH request (via curl or script) to '/rest/v1/devices/{id}' setting the username to 'srv_monitor' and password to 'SrvR00m2024!Secure'.

task_completion.description: Agent creates or writes to the specified log file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal commands (like echo, cat, or script execution) showing data being written to '/home/ga/camera_credential_update.log'.

task_completion.description: The log file contains correctly formatted entries for all three cameras.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible contents of the log file showing lines matching the exact format: '[ISO_TIMESTAMP] Updated credentials for "CAMERA_NAME" (ID: DEVICE_ID) - username set to: USERNAME'.

integrity.description: The agent actually interacted with the Nx Witness API rather than just faking the log file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing actual JSON responses from the API, or execution of a script that makes network requests to https://localhost:7001.

integrity.description: The device IDs in the log file appear to be genuine UUIDs retrieved from the API.
integrity.visual_evidence: The log file contents show real UUID-format strings (e.g., xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx) for the device IDs, not placeholder text like 'DEVICE_ID'.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply write a hardcoded log file without performing the required API updates.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of actual API request commands/scripts and their execution, rather than just a single command or script that only writes text to the log file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires interacting with the Nx Witness REST API at https://localhost:7001. Authentication is done via Bearer token from the /rest/v1/login/sessions endpoint using admin/Admin1234!. Device credentials are updated using PATCH requests to /rest/v1/devices/{id}. The log file must be at /home/ga/camera_credential_update.log and strictly follow the format: [ISO_TIMESTAMP] Updated credentials for "CAMERA_NAME" (ID: DEVICE_ID) - username set to: USERNAME. Device IDs are UUIDs retrieved from the API.
